title: renegotiation
text: renegotiation Enables the ability to renegotiate a secure connection. renegotiation | reneg = {yes| y | 1} | {no | n | 0} Use This setting can be used to toggle the ability to renegotiate a secure connection. Renegotiation is enabled by default, but can be overridden by setting this parameter to {no | n | 0}. Description Uniface has already disabled the legacy insecure re-negotiations that are prone to Man in the Middle (MITM) attacks. This setting gives the ability to turn off secure renegotiation as well. When this option is set to "no", all kinds of renegotiation is disabled. In that case, OpenSSL will not renegotiate the connection, and will always perform a new handshake, instead of renegotiating the connection parameters. Example: Disabling TLS/SSL renegotiation The following assignment settings will disable all kinds of renegotiation from this profile. Explain this code ;client.asn [NET_SETTINGS] CheckClient= renegotiation=no , client_certificate=clcert.crt, %\ client_key=clkey.key, client_key_password=pas5Word Copy Was this topic helpful?
